patches.find: A model that produces two different numbers for the same physical asymmetry, both labeled as confirmation, is not pointing — it is reading off whatever the data says. (3)
patches.replace: A model that produces two different numbers for the same physical asymmetry, both presented as supportive of the dome's NMP narrative, is not pointing — it is reading off whatever the data says. Even granting that W019's 5.5× is a single-week observation while WIN-043's 2.26× is presented as a multi-decade average, the dome publishes no methodology specifying which window WIN-043 corresponds to (the same dataset yields 1.69× over 1900–2025, 2.34× over 2020–2025, and 9.43× over 2010–2025); the dome cannot consistently say which ratio is the prediction. (3)
patches.rationale: Hole 1 (minor): 'both labeled as confirmation' misstates dome status taxonomy — WIN-043 is CONFIRMED, W019 is REFINED. Hole 2 (moderate): counter (2) missing time-window preempt. A dome defender will claim W019 single-week vs WIN-043 multi-decade average are not comparable. Adding the window-sensitivity clause converts 'contradiction' to 'unspecified-window equivocation' which the data already supports.

patches.find: per the post-2019 NMP slowdown (Chulliat et al. 2020)
patches.replace: per the post-2019 NMP slowdown (Livermore et al. 2020; Chulliat et al. 2020)
patches.rationale: Hole 3 (minor): Livermore et al. 2020 Nat. Geosci. 13, 387-391 is the more canonical citation for the post-2019 NMP slowdown; Chulliat 2020 is a model-update technical report not the primary slowdown paper.
